                  <jats:p>Since the 2019 novel coronavirus disease (COVID-19) outbreak in 2019 and the pandemic continues for more than one year, a vast amount of drug research has been conducted and few of them got FDA approval. Our objective is to prioritize repurposable drugs using a pipeline that systematically integrates the interaction between COVID-19 and drugs, deep graph neural networks, and in vitro/population-based validations. We first collected all
results.tldr: It is demonstrated that the integration of extensive interactions, deep neural networks, and multiple evidence can facilitate the rapid identification of candidate drugs for COVID-19 treatment.

results.title: Prediction of drug-drug interaction events using graph neural networks based feature extraction
results.abstract: <jats:title>Abstract</jats:title><jats:p>The prevalence of multi_drug therapies has been increasing in recent years, particularly among the elderly who are suffering from several diseases. However, unexpected Drug_Drug interaction (DDI) can cause adverse reactions or critical toxicity, which puts patients in danger. As the need for multi_drug treatment increases, it's becoming increasingly necessary to discover DDIs. Nevertheless, DDIs detection in an extensive number of drug pairs, both in-vitr

results.title: A computational approach to drug repurposing using graph neural networks
results.abstract: This work presents a graph neural network drug repurposing model, which is referred to as GDRnet, to efficiently screen a large database of approved drugs and predict the possible treatment for novel diseases and shows that many drugs from the predicted list are being studied for their efficacy against the disease.
results.tldr: This work presents a graph neural network drug repurposing model, which is referred to as GDRnet, to efficiently screen a large database of approved drugs and predict the possible treatment for novel diseases and shows that many drugs from the predicted list are being studied for their efficacy against the disease.

results.abstract: <jats:title>Abstract</jats:title><jats:p>Drug repurposing is an exciting field of research toward recognizing a new FDA-approved drug target for the treatment of a specific disease. It has received extensive attention regarding the tedious, time-consuming, and highly expensive procedure with a high risk of failure of new drug discovery. Data-driven approaches are an important class of methods that have been introduced for identifying a candidate drug against a target disease. In the present stud

results.title: Graph neural networks driven acceleration in drug discovery
results.tldr: This review focuses on the interdisciplinary integration of GNNs throughout the discovery process, including lead discovery and optimization, synthetic route design, drug–target interaction prediction, and molecular property profiling, while critically evaluating the challenges in translational medicine.
results.abstract: This review focuses on the interdisciplinary integration of GNNs throughout the discovery process, including lead discovery and optimization, synthetic route design, drug–target interaction prediction, and molecular property profiling, while critically evaluating the challenges in translational medicine.

results.abstract: BEHOR is presented, a more comprehensive version of the REDIRECTION model, which utilize the DISNET biomedical graph as the primary source of information, providing the model with extensive and intricate data to tackle the drug repurposing challenge.
results.tldr: BEHOR is presented, a more comprehensive version of the REDIRECTION model, which utilize the DISNET biomedical graph as the primary source of information, providing the model with extensive and intricate data to tackle the drug repurposing challenge.

results.title: The use of knowledge graphs for drug repurposing: From classical machine learning algorithms to graph neural networks

results.abstract: The motivation for adopting KG-based knowledge representations, traditional machine learning and deep learning approaches are discussed, followed by an analysis of selected tools, their construction, link prediction capabilities, and inherent advantages and limitations.

results.title: Improving drug-induced liver injury prediction using graph neural networks with augmented graph features from molecular optimisation
results.tldr: A novel approach that creates a custom graph dataset, driven by molecular optimisation, that incorporates detailed and realistic chemical features such as bond lengths and partial charges as input into the GNN models, and outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in DILI prediction.
results.abstract: A novel approach that creates a custom graph dataset, driven by molecular optimisation, that incorporates detailed and realistic chemical features such as bond lengths and partial charges as input into the GNN models, and outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in DILI prediction.

results.title: Drug Therapeutic-Use Class Prediction and Repurposing Using Graph Convolutional Networks
results.abstract: <jats:p>An important stage in the process of discovering new drugs is when candidate molecules are tested of their efficacy. It is reported that testing drug efficacy empirically costs billions of dollars in the drug discovery pipeline. As a mechanism of expediting this process, researchers have resorted to using computational methods to predict the action of molecules in silico. Here, we present a way of predicting the therapeutic-use class of drugs from chemical structures only using graph con

results.title: Network-based drug repurposing for psychiatric disorders using single-cell genomics
results.tldr: This work integrated population-scale single-cell genomics data and analyzed cell-type-level gene regulatory networks across schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and autism to reveal potential druggable transcription factors co-regulating known risk genes that converge into cell-type-specific co-regulated modules.
results.abstract: This work integrated population-scale single-cell genomics data and analyzed cell-type-level gene regulatory networks across schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and autism to reveal potential druggable transcription factors co-regulating known risk genes that converge into cell-type-specific co-regulated modules.

results.title: Enhancing drug-target interaction prediction with graph representation learning and knowledge-based regularization
results.tldr: A novel framework that combines graph neural networks with knowledge integration for DTI prediction that integrates domain knowledge from biomedical ontologies and databases using a knowledge-based regularization strategy to infuse biological context into the learned representations.
results.abstract: A novel framework that combines graph neural networks with knowledge integration for DTI prediction that integrates domain knowledge from biomedical ontologies and databases using a knowledge-based regularization strategy to infuse biological context into the learned representations.

results.abstract: This article investigates multi-modal gene disease and disease drug networks via link prediction algorithms to select drugs for repurposing to treat skeletal muscle atrophy using graph convolutional networks and link prediction methods.

results.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Leishmanisis, a neglected tropical disease caused by protozoan parasites of the genus
                  <jats:italic>Leishmania</jats:italic>
                  , affects millions of individuals living in poverty across the world and is second to malaria in parasitic causes of death. Although current drugs for treating leishmaniasis exist, these are either highly toxic, ineffective, or expensive. For this reason, there i
results.tldr: A deep learning model for drug repurposing based on hyperbolic graph neural networks is presented that proposes existing drugs with the potential to treat leishmaniasis and suggests two existing drugs that could target essential pathways for growth and survival of Leishmania parasites.

Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) is a significant concern in drug development, often leading to the discontinuation of clinical trials and the withdrawal of drugs from the market due to severe hepatotoxicity. This study explores the application of graph neural networks (GNNs) for DILI prediction, using molecular graph representations as the primary input.    METHODS. results.title: Graph Neural Network-Based Approaches to Drug Repurposing: A Comprehensive Survey
results.abstract: <jats:p>Drug development is time-consuming and costly, with many efforts failing. About 90% of new molecules synthesized never reach the market, most of which have already undergone laboratory and animal testing phases. Drug repurposing, the use of drugs that successfully passed clinical trials for non-original purposes, reduces drug development time and cost. It is crucial for rare diseases and emerging diseases like COVID-19. Rare diseases lack sufficient incentives due to limited patient popu

results.title: Over-Squashing in GNNs and Causal Inference of Rewiring Strategies
results.tldr: These findings suggest that rewiring is most beneficial when over-squashing is both substantial and corrected with restraint -- while overly aggressive rewiring, or rewiring applied to minimally over-squashed graphs, is unlikely to help and may even harm performance.
results.abstract: These findings suggest that rewiring is most beneficial when over-squashing is both substantial and corrected with restraint -- while overly aggressive rewiring, or rewiring applied to minimally over-squashed graphs, is unlikely to help and may even harm performance.

results.tldr: Overall, XAIPath offers a scalable and explainable approach for identifying drug–disease associations, facilitating hypothesis generation and biological validation, and highlighting the value of explainable AI in biomedical research and drug repurposing.

results.abstract: <jats:p>The discovery of new drugs is a time-consuming, expensive, and uncertain task. Drug repurposing is an efficient solution to this problem, which aims to find new uses for existing drugs. This paper introduces an AI-powered framework for computational drug repurposing based on Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for predicting disease-drug associations. The biomedical knowledge graph is represented as a heterogeneous graph of diseases, genes, and drugs, which are linked by validated biological in
results.tldr: This paper introduces an AI-powered framework for computational drug repurposing based on Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for predicting disease-drug associations and indicates a scalable and biologically informed approach to accelerate drug repurposing.

results.title: Multi-Task Graph Neural Network For Alzheimer’s Disease Drug Repurposing Using Knowledge Graph And Multi-Level Evidence
results.abstract: Developing drugs for treating Alzheimer’s disease (AD) has been extremely challenging and costly due to limited knowledge on underlying biological mechanisms and therapeutic targets. Repurposing drugs or their combination has shown potential in accelerating drug development due to the reduced drug toxicity while targeting multiple pathologies. results.venue: American Journal of Artificial Intelligence and Neural Networks
results.abstract: <jats:p>The process of discovering novel pharmaceutical  compounds is traditionally a capital-intensive and time-consuming  endeavor, often plagued by high attrition rates in late-stage clinical  trials. Structure-based drug design has emerged as a pivotal  methodology to mitigate these risks by leveraging the three dimensional information of target proteins. results.venue: International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications
results.tldr: A machine learning pipeline that uses unsupervised deep embedded clustering, combined with supervised graph neural network link prediction to identify new drug-disease links from multi-omic data to provide new drug-disease link prospects across unrelated disease domains is proposed.
results.abstract: A machine learning pipeline that uses unsupervised deep embedded clustering, combined with supervised graph neural network link prediction to identify new drug-disease links from multi-omic data to provide new drug-disease link prospects across unrelated disease domains is proposed.

results.title: CADG-DTA: a deep learning model for drug-target binding affinity prediction, using graph neural networks and cross-attention fusion
results.tldr: CADG-DTA is introduced, a novel approach that integrates atom-level and residue-level graphs for drugs and targets, respectively, allowing for the extraction of intricate structural features using graph neural networks and enhances interpretability and predictive accuracy by assigning weights to various substructures, unlike previous methods that rely on simple concatenation.
results.abstract: CADG-DTA is introduced, a novel approach that integrates atom-level and residue-level graphs for drugs and targets, respectively, allowing for the extraction of intricate structural features using graph neural networks and enhances interpretability and predictive accuracy by assigning weights to various substructures, unlike previous methods that rely on simple concatenation.

results.title: Medical Drug Analysis using graph neural network
results.venue: 2024 IEEE International Students' Conference on Electrical, Electronics and Computer Science (SCEECS)

results.title: Utilizing Large Language Models and Graph Neural Networks for Protein–Protein Interaction Prediction and Isozyme Estimation
results.venue: 2025 2nd International Generative AI and Computational Language Modelling Conference (GACLM)

results.title: Drug Repurposing Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning: A Systematic Literature Review.
results.tldr: To achieve real-world medical impact, future research must prioritize interpretable architectures, advanced deep learning frameworks, and robust hybrid models, particularly for treating rare diseases, to bridge the gap between computational success and clinical implementation effectively.
results.abstract: To achieve real-world medical impact, future research must prioritize interpretable architectures, advanced deep learning frameworks, and robust hybrid models, particularly for treating rare diseases, to bridge the gap between computational success and clinical implementation effectively.

results.title: Graph Neural Network-based Drug-Target Interaction Prediction for Precision Medicine
results.tldr: A Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based DTI prediction framework that represents drugs and targets as nodes in a bipartite biological graph and learns interaction-aware embeddings through message passing is proposed.
results.abstract: A Graph Neural Network (GNN)-based DTI prediction framework that represents drugs and targets as nodes in a bipartite biological graph and learns interaction-aware embeddings through message passing is proposed.

results.title: Identification of targets for drug repurposing to treat COVID-19 using a Deep Learning Neural Network
results.tldr: The results suggest that the model has the potential to facilitate drug repurposing and aid in the fight against COVID-19, and enables rapid analysis of disease-related human protein interaction networks and prediction of candidate drug targets from a large-scale knowledge graph in a cost-effective and efficient manner.
results.abstract: The results suggest that the model has the potential to facilitate drug repurposing and aid in the fight against COVID-19, and enables rapid analysis of disease-related human protein interaction networks and prediction of candidate drug targets from a large-scale knowledge graph in a cost-effective and efficient manner.

results.title: A Hybrid PKPD-Machine Learning Framework for Precision Dosing and Drug Repurposing: Advancing AI Applications in Personalized Pharmacotherapy
results.venue: 2025 3rd DMIHER International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Education and Industry (IDICAIHEI)

results.title: BiProSimGCN: Prediction of Drugs-Proteins Interactions Using Graph Convolutional Neural Network and Proteins Similarity
results.venue: 2023 IEEE Asia-Pacific Conference on Computer Science and Data Engineering (CSDE)

results.title: Generative AI in drug repurposing and biomarker discovery: a multimodal approach
results.tldr: Heterogeneous attention-based meta-learning graph neural network named HAMGNN offers a generalized, biologically grounded, and unified framework for evidence-based drug repurposing and biomarker discovery in complex and emerging diseases.
results.abstract: Heterogeneous attention-based meta-learning graph neural network named HAMGNN offers a generalized, biologically grounded, and unified framework for evidence-based drug repurposing and biomarker discovery in complex and emerging diseases.

results.title: Overcoming Topology Bias and Cold-Start Limitations in Drug Repurposing: A Clinical-Outcome-Aligned LLM Framework
results.tldr: This framework proposes a framework that shifts the optimization objective from graph topology to clinical utility, integrating Knowledge Graph RAG, Supervised Fine-Tuning, and Kahneman-Tversky Optimization using Phase III clinical trial outcomes as rewards, and successfully bridges the gap between semantic inference, topological structure, and clinical reality.
results.abstract: This framework proposes a framework that shifts the optimization objective from graph topology to clinical utility, integrating Knowledge Graph RAG, Supervised Fine-Tuning, and Kahneman-Tversky Optimization using Phase III clinical trial outcomes as rewards, and successfully bridges the gap between semantic inference, topological structure, and clinical reality.

results.title: Drug repurposing of fluvastatin as a DP2 antagonist: an ensemble learning strategy with in vitro cellular functional validation.
results.tldr: The developed workflow demonstrates effective performance in the discovery of DP2 antagonists, offering a valuable reference for AI-driven drug repositioning targeting this receptor.
results.abstract: The developed workflow demonstrates effective performance in the discovery of DP2 antagonists, offering a valuable reference for AI-driven drug repositioning targeting this receptor.

results.title: Predicting Drug Efficacy with Molecular Embeddings and Graph Context
results.venue: 2025 6th International Conference on IoT Based Control Networks and Intelligent Systems (ICICNIS)

results.title: Transforming Healthcare and Accelerating Drug Discovery and Personalized Treatment Analysis using AI
results.venue: International Research Journal on Advanced Engineering and Management (IRJAEM)
results.tldr: This work synthesizes current methodologies, demonstrates practical applications using real-world data models, and addresses critical challenges including data quality, regulatory compliance, and model interpretability, establishing AI as an indispensable tool for modern pharmaceutical innovation.
results.abstract: This work synthesizes current methodologies, demonstrates practical applications using real-world data models, and addresses critical challenges including data quality, regulatory compliance, and model interpretability, establishing AI as an indispensable tool for modern pharmaceutical innovation.

results.title: Using the Heterogeneous Network's Self-Adaptive Topological Structure for Drug-Protein Interaction Prediction
results.venue: 2024 Global Conference on Communications and Information Technologies (GCCIT)

results.title: Riemann-GNN: Causal Reasoning on Hyperbolic Riemannian Manifolds for Interpretable Drug-Disease Prediction
results.tldr: In a case study on amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Riemann-GNN identifies 20 novel candidate drugs and provides interpretable causal paths explaining their therapeutic relevance, demonstrating the utility of integrating hyperbolic representation learning with causal semantics for robust and explainable biomedical AI.
results.abstract: In a case study on amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Riemann-GNN identifies 20 novel candidate drugs and provides interpretable causal paths explaining their therapeutic relevance, demonstrating the utility of integrating hyperbolic representation learning with causal semantics for robust and explainable biomedical AI.

results.title: Improved half-maximal inhibitory concentration regression model using amyotrophic lateral sclerosis data
results.venue: Bulletin of Electrical Engineering and Informatics
results.tldr: Three variants of graph neural networks (GNN) namely graph attention networks (GAT), message passing neural networks, and graph isomorphism networks are utilized in the proposed work to produce precise half-maximal inhibitory concentration regression models in ALS.
results.abstract: Three variants of graph neural networks (GNN) namely graph attention networks (GAT), message passing neural networks, and graph isomorphism networks are utilized in the proposed work to produce precise half-maximal inhibitory concentration regression models in ALS.

results.title: Machine learning-driven drug repurposing for GPR17: activity prediction via graph neural networks and multistage computational validation.
results.venue: Journal of Biomolecular Structure and Dynamics
results.tldr: An integrated computational workflow combining graph neural network (GNN)-based prediction with multistage structure-based validation provides a practical strategy for early-stage GPR17 ligand discovery and future CNS-oriented drug development.
results.abstract: An integrated computational workflow combining graph neural network (GNN)-based prediction with multistage structure-based validation provides a practical strategy for early-stage GPR17 ligand discovery and future CNS-oriented drug development.
